name: velocity_exactly_at_threshold_arms
description: velocity == para_threshold exactly. Inline logic uses >= (not >), so this boundary case MUST arm. Pins the inclusive boundary. A wrong impl using > instead of >= would yield should_arm=False here and fail this fixture.
derivation: velocity = current_return - prev_return = 1.5 - 1.0 = 0.5. should_arm = (velocity >= para_threshold) AND (not currently_armed) = (0.5 >= 0.5) AND (not False) = True AND True = True. Note: inline uses '>=', so exact-equality arms.
